illusionConnectReCodes.title: Illusion Connect Re Codes
illusionConnectReCodes.intro: Use Illusion Connect Re codes to pick up launch rewards, social event gifts, and quick resource boosts. Enter each code exactly as shown from the in-game redeem menu.
illusionConnectReCodes.stepsTitle: How to Redeem Illusion Connect Re Codes
steps: Launch Illusion Connect: Re and finish the opening tutorial until the main lobby is unlocked.
Tap your avatar in the top-left corner to open the account menu.
Enter Settings and choose the Redeem option.
Paste the code exactly as shown, then confirm the submission.
Open your mailbox or reward pop-up and claim the items immediately.

illusionConnectReTierList.title: Illusion Connect Re Tier List
illusionConnectReTierList.intro: This Illusion Connect Re tier list focuses on launch-week value for rerolls, early story progress, and stable team building rather than long-tail niche modes.

tiers.name: S Tier
tiers.description: These Radiants deliver the strongest immediate account power when you are pushing story and clearing wave-heavy stages.

characters.name: Yuffie
characters.bestFor: Wave clears and fast campaign progress
characters.summary: Yuffie is the strongest early damage chase because her burst clears multi-enemy boards quickly and speeds up reroll payoffs.

characters.name: Anna
characters.summary: Anna keeps beginner teams alive through long stages and gives immediate value in any stable launch roster.

tiers.name: A Tier
tiers.description: These picks stay strong if you want a balanced start without rerolling forever for a perfect account.

characters.name: Miyuki
characters.bestFor: Daily story farming and flexible team cores
characters.summary: Miyuki offers dependable damage output and fits both reroll accounts and login-reward progression plans.

characters.name: Kiraya
characters.bestFor: Frontline stability and protecting your backline
characters.summary: Kiraya is one of the safest launch tanks because she buys time for your carries and keeps the leader safer.

tiers.name: Support Core
tiers.description: A strong start still needs role coverage, so build around damage, sustain, and frontline control instead of chasing one carry only.

characters.name: AoE damage slot
characters.summary: Open with a dependable AoE unit to keep clears fast and energy-efficient.

characters.name: Healer or shield slot
characters.summary: One sustain slot stabilizes your run far more than stacking another fragile damage dealer.

illusionConnectReRerollGuide.title: Illusion Connect Re Reroll Guide
illusionConnectReRerollGuide.intro: An efficient Illusion Connect Re reroll route should minimize menu downtime, use the earliest summons, and stop once your opening roster already supports campaign progress.
illusionConnectReRerollGuide.targetsTitle: Illusion Connect Re Best Starter Targets
illusionConnectReRerollGuide.stopTitle: When to Stop an Illusion Connect Re Reroll

steps.title: Finish the tutorial fast
steps.description: Skip every optional dialogue scene, clear the opening combat sequence, and unlock the first lobby rewards before checking banners.

steps.title: Collect all launch mail
steps.description: Claim pre-registration bonuses, starter gifts, and any launch mailbox rewards before you spend your first batch of pulls.

steps.title: Pull on the highest-value opening banner
steps.description: Use the banner that gives the fastest access to a top damage dealer or healer instead of splitting resources across multiple early banners.

steps.title: Reset quickly if the account misses
steps.description: If the first pull cycle does not give at least one premium carry or sustain piece, restart immediately rather than trying to repair a weak opening.

targets.name: Yuffie
targets.reason: Fastest story-clear payoff and a strong reason to keep the account.

targets.name: Anna
targets.reason: Adds immediate roster stability and improves event survivability.

targets.name: Miyuki
targets.reason: A dependable fallback if you want a balanced account without overextending reroll time.

stopRules: Stop when you open with one premium damage dealer and one reliable sustain piece.
Stop if the account has Yuffie or Anna plus enough early currency to keep progressing.
Stop once the roster can cover frontline, AoE damage, and healing for the first story chapters.

illusionConnectReBeginnerGuide.title: Illusion Connect Re Beginner Guide
illusionConnectReBeginnerGuide.intro: Illusion Connect Re rewards clean first-day planning. Focus on story unlocks, formation basics, and efficient upgrade spending before you spread resources across every Radiant.

steps.title: Push story until your daily systems unlock
steps.description: The first story chapters unlock the menus and reward loops that matter most, so early stamina should go into campaign progress first.

steps.title: Build one stable frontline core
steps.description: Pick your best tank or shield unit early and keep your leader protected while your backline carries do the damage.

steps.title: Invest in a small main squad
steps.description: Upgrade one compact team instead of spreading materials across too many launch pulls. Concentrated upgrades clear content faster.

steps.title: Use bonds and home features for efficient growth
steps.description: Bond progression and Dream Home systems add useful account value, so fold them into your daily loop instead of treating them as pure side content.

quickTips: Save premium currency for banners that improve your main story lineup rather than every launch banner.
Upgrade one carry, one sustain unit, and one frontline protector before investing in niche backups.
Check event missions every day because launch rewards often overlap with the story path you already need to clear.
If progress stalls, revisit formation order and deployment timing before assuming you need more raw power.

illusionConnectReTeams.title: Illusion Connect Re Teams
illusionConnectReTeams.intro: The best Illusion Connect Re teams at launch are balanced, role-complete lineups that protect the leader, keep costs manageable, and let carries fire ultimates on time.
illusionConnectReTeams.lineupTitle: Illusion Connect Re Balanced Launch Lineup

cards.name: Frontline anchor
cards.description: Use one sturdy tank or shield specialist to absorb pressure and stop your core damage dealers from getting collapsed early.

cards.name: Primary damage pair
cards.description: Blend one AoE clearer with one consistent single-target or sustained damage unit so the team handles both waves and elites.

cards.name: Sustain and utility
cards.description: A healer or shield support keeps the squad consistent and makes event stages much safer than replacing the slot with a fourth attacker.

lineup.reason: Provides the defensive cushion your backline needs in early boss and elite stages.
Clears enemy waves quickly and turns story stamina into faster progress.
Stabilizes long fights and keeps launch rosters from collapsing after one bad trade.

lineup.pick: Miyuki or your best SSR carry
lineup.reason: Lets you adapt around the pulls you actually have while keeping your main damage pace high.

illusionConnectReCombatGuide.title: Illusion Connect Re Combat Guide
illusionConnectReCombatGuide.intro: Illusion Connect Re combat gets smoother once you treat positioning, deployment cost, and ultimate timing as one system instead of isolated buttons.

topics.name: Formation control
topics.description: The 3x3 field rewards clean lane management and deliberate frontline placement.
points: Protect the leader by placing your sturdiest unit in the lane receiving the heaviest pressure.
Do not overstack one row unless the enemy pattern leaves the opposite side open.
Rebuild a broken line quickly so backline Radiants are not forced to tank.

topics.name: Deployment rhythm
topics.description: Winning early fights is often about spending cost at the right moment instead of dumping every unit as soon as it is ready.
points: Hold enough cost to answer enemy threats instead of opening with every damage unit immediately.
Drop sustain before the team fully collapses so heals and shields have time to matter.
Use ultimates to swing wave transitions rather than wasting them on nearly defeated targets.

topics.name: Nightmares and stage mechanics
topics.description: Special enemies and mechanics punish autopilot play, so adjust your lineup when a stage keeps blocking progress.
points: Swap in more sustain or control if the stage pressure outlasts your burst window.
Use stronger wave clear when Nightmares spawn supporting mobs around a priority target.
Treat repeat failures as a team-structure problem first, not just a raw level issue.

topics.name: Manual improvement loop
topics.description: A quick review after every failed stage helps you fix the right layer of the team.
points: Check whether the frontline broke too early.
Check whether your energy and cost pacing delayed a key heal or burst.
Check whether the carry order matches the enemy wave pattern.

illusionConnectReEvents.title: Illusion Connect Re Events
illusionConnectReEvents.intro: Illusion Connect Re launch rewards are split across pre-registration milestones, login tracks, social rewards, and event task ladders, so keeping them separate from redeem codes helps players claim everything faster.
illusionConnectReEvents.checklistTitle: Illusion Connect Re Launch Reward Checklist

cards.name: Pre-registration rewards
cards.label: Milestone rewards
cards.description: Claim the account-wide launch bonuses tied to the global pre-registration campaign as soon as the mailbox opens.

cards.name: Launch login program
cards.label: Daily login
cards.description: Log in every day during the launch window so you do not miss premium pulls, upgrade items, and free Radiant progression value.

cards.name: Social gift campaigns
cards.label: Community events
cards.description: Watch the official Discord and Facebook channels for code drops, event notices, and reward announcements tied to launch activity.

cards.name: Story and mission events
cards.label: Progress rewards
cards.description: Push campaign chapters and event missions together because the first-week reward tracks often overlap with the same gameplay loop.

checklist: Claim pre-registration mail before spending your first launch pulls.
Finish each day’s login bonus during the opening event window.
Check official social channels for extra code drops and campaign notices.
Clear the matching event missions while you push the main story route.
Review limited-time reward tabs before server reset so nothing expires unclaimed.
Use event rewards to strengthen your main launch squad instead of scattering them across bench units.

faq.title: Illusion Connect Re
faq.subtitle: Quick answers for the most common Illusion Connect Re launch questions.

questions.question: What is Illusion Connect Re?
questions.answer: Illusion Connect Re is an anime strategy RPG built around real-time combat, Radiant team building, bond progression, and Dream Home account systems.

questions.question: How do Illusion Connect Re codes work?
questions.answer: Illusion Connect Re codes are entered through the in-game Redeem menu after the tutorial unlocks the main lobby account options.

questions.question: Is Illusion Connect Re rerolling worth it?
questions.answer: Illusion Connect Re rerolling is worth it if you want a stronger launch start, especially when you are aiming for a premium AoE carry or a top healer.

questions.question: What makes a strong Illusion Connect Re team?
questions.answer: A strong Illusion Connect Re team combines one frontline anchor, one reliable sustain option, and enough damage to clear both waves and elite targets.

questions.question: Where can I download Illusion Connect Re?
questions.answer: Illusion Connect Re is available from the official Google Play and App Store pages for Android and iOS players.

questions.question: Which Illusion Connect Re events matter most at launch?
questions.answer: The most important Illusion Connect Re launch events are the pre-registration rewards, login tracks, social reward drops, and story-linked event missions.
